CRM.rationale: Rich valuation (71.7x PE, 8.5x P/S) on decelerating growth and weak 6.9% ROE after a 53% one-year run creates unfavorable risk/reward despite quality fundamentals and a clean balance sheet.
key_factors: PE diluted at 71.71x — near the low end of a 70.1–1063.7x 1y range but still very elevated in absolute terms
P/S at 8.502x and P/B at 4.969x confirm premium pricing
Quarterly revenue trajectory decelerating: Q2 FY24 $8.60B → Q3 $8.72B (+1.4%) → Q4 $9.29B (+6.5% seq)
ROE only 6.9% despite 11.9% net margin — capital efficiency is poor given $59.65B equity base
Stock already up +53.2% over 1 year and +13.4% over 3 months — momentum may be exhausted
2024-03-23 Seeking Alpha downgrade to Hold explicitly citing overvaluation

sources_cited.excerpt: Salesforce: Currently Overvalued And It Requires A Lot To Revert It (Downgrading To Hold)

AMZN.rationale: At 62x P/E following an 80% one-year rally, AMZN's risk-adjusted reward is balanced-to-slightly-negative: fundamentals are strong and accelerating, but the stretched multiple and priced-in optimism cap upside while leaving meaningful drawdown risk.
key_factors: P/E of 62.2x is elevated despite being near the low end of the 1-year range (57.6-326.2), limiting multiple expansion runway
1-year price return of +79.9% suggests much of the AI/AWS/advertising optimism is already priced in
Revenue acceleration is real: Q4 2023 rev=$169.96B vs Q2 2023 rev=$134.38B, with FY2023 revenue=$574.78B and EPS=$2.90
Fundamentals are solid but not exceptional enough to justify 62x P/E: net_margin=5.3%, ROE=15.1%, operating_margin=6.4%
Bullish news flow on AI and Magnificent Seven positioning is already reflected in the 80% rally
